meta_title: For Freelancers: freelance pitching to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching: Overcome Project Stall for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on freelance projects. Use this intermediate checklist to master freelance pitching and rapid delivery with Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of scope control in your pitch. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform your freelance pitching from a 'wish list' into a structured, executable roadmap that guarantees delivery and client satisfaction.
content.intro:
The graveyard of freelance careers is paved with half-finished repositories and abandoned GitHub branches. You started with a vision of high-ticket AI automation, but now you're drowning in scope creep and technical debt. The feeling of 'almost done' has become a permanent state, eroding your credibility and your bottom line.

It is time to pivot from being a coder who tinkers to a builder who ships. By tightening your freelance pitching process to align with modular AI architecture, you stop selling 'solutions' and start selling 'outcomes.' We are going to deploy the power of Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your development cycle into a high-velocity, repeatable engine.

steps.title: Define the 'Minimum Viable Pitch'
steps.description: Stop pitching features and start pitching milestones. Use Antigravity to map the core logic before writing a single line of production code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a modular project structure for a [Project Type] that delivers core value in 14 days. Exclude non-essential features and define the MVP scope.
steps.claude_code_command: claude -c 'Create a project README and folder structure based on the Antigravity architecture plan.'

steps.title: Automate Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. This removes the friction of starting and keeps you focused on the unique business logic your client paid for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for [Tech Stack] including testing suites and CI/CD pipelines.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Initialize the boilerplate generated by Antigravity and run the initial test suite.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of API integrations. This prevents you from getting bogged down in boilerplate API handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an integration pattern for [API Name] that handles authentication, rate limiting, and error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Implement the API integration logic into the existing service layer.'

steps.title: Execute Rapid Refinement
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on feedback faster than your client can provide it. Keep the loop tight to prevent project stagnation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current codebase for better readability and performance based on [Feedback/Constraint].
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Refactor the service layer to improve modularity and address the latest feedback.'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Handover
steps.description: Automate the documentation and deployment process to signify the end of the project. A clean exit is as important as a strong start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive technical handover document for the client including deployment steps and maintenance instructions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate a doc/HANDOVER.md file and verify that the deployment script is ready for production.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Maintenance Buffer' in your pitch; it protects you from endless minor requests.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write; it saves you hours of manual documentation at the end.
Keep your Antigravity project maps in your repo root; it helps clients understand your technical maturity.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement via Claude Code, break it into three smaller modules immediately.
Use your 'finished' projects as templates in Antigravity to create a private library of successful delivery patterns.
content.next_steps: Review your current stalled project, run it through the Antigravity architect tool, and use Claude Code to complete the next milestone by EOD.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to stop stalling and start shipping high-value AI solutions. The difference between a struggling freelancer and a top-tier builder is the ability to maintain momentum through structured tooling. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
